001package guiMultipleRoleDispatch;
002
003import java.util.ArrayList;
004import java.util.List;
005import javafx.collections.FXCollections;
006import javafx.geometry.Pos;
007import javafx.scene.Scene;
008import javafx.scene.control.Button;
009import javafx.scene.control.ComboBox;
010import javafx.scene.control.Label;
011import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
012import javafx.scene.shape.Line;
013import javafx.scene.text.Font;
014import javafx.stage.Stage;
015import database.Database;
016import entityClasses.User;
017
018/*******
019 * <p> Title: GUIMultipleRoleDispatchPage Class. </p>
020 * 
021 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based Admin Update Page.</p>
022 * 
023 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
024 * 
025 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
026 * 
027 * @version 1.00                2025-08-20 Initial version
028 *  
029 */
030
031public class ViewMultipleRoleDispatch {
032
033        /*-****************************************************************************************
034
035                Attributes
036
037         */
038
039        // These are the application values required by the user interface
040
041        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
042        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
043
044
045        // These are the widget attributes for the GUI. There are 3 areas for this GUI.
046
047        // GUI Area 1: It informs the user about the purpose of this page and whose account is being
048        // used. There is no button to allow this user to update the account settings.
049        private static Label label_PageTitle = new Label("Multiple Role Dispatch Page");
050        private static Label label_UserDetails = new Label();
051        
052        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
053        private static Line line_Separator1 = new Line(20, 95, width-20, 95);
054
055        // GUI Area 2: This area consists of a label to ask the user what roles to place, a ComboBox 
056        // so the user can select the role, and a button to perform that role.
057        private static Label label_WhichRole = new Label("Which role do you wish to play:");
058        protected static ComboBox <String> combobox_SelectRole = new ComboBox <String>();
059        private static Button button_PerformRole = new Button("Perform Role");          
060        
061        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
062        private static Line line_Separator4 = new Line(20, 525, width-20,525);
063
064        // GUI Area 3: This is last of the GUI areas.  It is used for quitting the application and
065        // logging out.
066        private static Button button_Logout = new Button("Logout");
067        private static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
068
069        // This is the end of the GUI objects for the page.
070        
071        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
072
073        private static ViewMultipleRoleDispatch theView;        // Used to determine if instantiation of
074                                                                                                                // the class is needed
075
076
077        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
078        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
079
080        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
081        private static Pane theRootPane;                        // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
082        protected static User theUser;                          // The current user of the application
083
084        private static Scene theMultipleRoleDispatchScene = null;       
085
086
087        /*-********************************************************************************************
088
089        Constructor
090
091         */
092
093        public static void displayMultipleRoleDispatch(Stage ps, User user) {
094                
095                // Establish the references to the GUI and the current user
096                theStage = ps;
097                theUser = user;
098                
099                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewMultipleRoleDispatch();
100
101                List<String> list = new ArrayList<String>();    // Create a new list empty list
102                theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(theUser.getUserName());
103                
104                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName() + "   Select which role");
105                setupLabelUI(label_UserDetails, "Arial", 20, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 50);
106
107                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName());
108                setupLabelUI(label_UserDetails, "Arial", 20, width, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 55);                 
109
110                System.out.println("*** Getting multiple role details for user: " + theUser.getUserName());
111                list = new ArrayList<String>();
112                list.add("<Select a role>");
113                if (theDatabase.getCurrentAdminRole()) list.add("Admin");
114                if (theDatabase.getCurrentStudentRole()) list.add("Student");
115                if (theDatabase.getCurrentStaffRole()) list.add("Staff");
116                combobox_SelectRole.setItems(FXCollections.observableArrayList(list));
117
118                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
119                combobox_SelectRole.getSelectionModel().select(0);
120                
121                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
122                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: Multiple Role Dispatch");   
123                theStage.setScene(theMultipleRoleDispatchScene);                // Set this page onto the stage
124                theStage.show();                                                                                // Display it to the user
125        }
126
127
128        /**********
129         * <p> Method: ViewMultipleRoleDispatch() </p>
130         * 
131         * <p> Description: This method initializes all the elements of the graphical user interface.
132         * This method determines the location, size, font, color, and change and event handlers for
133         * each GUI object. </p>
134         * 
135         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
136         * 
137         * @param user specifies the User for this GUI and it's methods
138         * 
139         */
140        private ViewMultipleRoleDispatch() {
141
142                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
143                theRootPane = new Pane();
144                theMultipleRoleDispatchScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
145
146                // Populate the window with the title and other common widgets and set their static state
147                
148                // GUI Area 1
149                setupLabelUI(label_PageTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 5);
150
151                // GUI Area 2
152                setupLabelUI(label_WhichRole, "Arial", 20, 200, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 110);
153
154                setupComboBoxUI(combobox_SelectRole, "Dialog", 16, 100, 305, 105);
155
156                setupButtonUI(button_PerformRole, "Dialog", 16, 100, Pos.CENTER, 495, 105);
157                button_PerformRole.setOnAction((_) -> 
158                {guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch.performRole(); });
159
160                
161                // GUI Area 3           
162                setupButtonUI(button_Logout, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 540);
163                button_Logout.setOnAction((_) -> 
164                {guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch.performLogout(); });
165
166                setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 540);
167                button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> 
168                {guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch.performQuit(); });
169                
170                // This is the end of the GUI Widgets for the page
171
172                // Place all of the just-initialized GUI elements into the pane
173                theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
174                                label_PageTitle,
175                                label_UserDetails,
176                                line_Separator1,
177                                label_WhichRole,
178                                combobox_SelectRole,
179                                button_PerformRole,
180                                line_Separator4, 
181                                button_Logout,
182                                button_Quit);
183        }
184
185
186        /*-********************************************************************************************
187
188                Helper methods to reduce code length
189
190         */
191
192        /**********
193         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
194         */
195
196        private static void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
197                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
198                l.setMinWidth(w);
199                l.setAlignment(p);
200                l.setLayoutX(x);
201                l.setLayoutY(y);                
202        }
203
204
205        /**********
206         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
207         * 
208         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
209         * @param ff    The font to be used
210         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
211         * @param w             The width of the Button
212         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
213         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
214         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
215         */
216        private static void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
217                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
218                b.setMinWidth(w);
219                b.setAlignment(p);
220                b.setLayoutX(x);
221                b.setLayoutY(y);                
222        }
223
224
225        /**********
226         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a ComboBox
227         * 
228         * @param c             The ComboBox object to be initialized
229         * @param ff    The font to be used
230         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
231         * @param w             The width of the ComboBox
232         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
233         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
234         */
235        private void setupComboBoxUI(ComboBox <String> c, String ff, double f, double w, double x, 
236                        double y){
237                c.setStyle("-fx-font: " + f + " " + ff + ";");
238                c.setMinWidth(w);
239                c.setLayoutX(x);
240                c.setLayoutY(y);
241        }
242}
